channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Microsoft Store Update: Third-Party Apps to Get Automatic Updates
item.description: Microsoft is updating its Microsoft Store to include automatic updates for third-party apps, making it easier to manage all app updates in one place.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Get started with Azure AI Content Understanding
item.description: Modern AI is about a lot more than chatbots, as shown by Microsoft’s Ignite 2024 pivot to using its stable of large and small language models to power autonomous agents.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Chinese online retailer Temu suspended in Vietnam
item.description: Chinese online retailer Temu has been told to suspend operations in Vietnam after it failed to meet an end-of-November deadline to register with the country's Ministry of Industry and Trade.
item.dc:creator: Aniruddha Ghosal, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: JPMorgan Chase cimenta su estrategia de IA sobre AWS
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. ha desplegado un asistente interno de IA apodado LLM Suite a más de 200.000 empleados.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trasformazione Digitale: 5 Tecnologie Fondamentali Spesso Sottovalutate
item.description: Ecco le 5 tecnologie più importanti per la trasformazione digitale, spesso ignorate a favore di elementi più nuovi e brillanti.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta to build $10 billion AI data center in Louisiana as Elon Musk expands his Tennessee AI facility
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The largest artificial intelligence data center ever built by Facebook’s parent company Meta is coming to northeast Louisiana, the company said Wednesday, bringing hopes that the $10 billion facility will transform an economically neglected corner of the state. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK and ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: China-Linked Hack Affects U.S. Telecom Firms and Dozens of Nations
item.description: A Chinese hacking campaign has been linked to at least eight U.S. telecom firms and dozens of nations, affecting private communications of senior government officials.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: For news, algorithmic social networks are a failed experiment
item.description: In 2016, when Twitter replaced its purely chronological timeline with one that used an algorithm to determine what users saw, I declared it to be a minor whoop at most—a 2.35 on a scale of 1 to 10.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: District of Columbia says Amazon secretly stopped fast deliveries to 2 predominantly Black ZIP codes
item.description: The District of Columbia sued Amazon on Wednesday, alleging the company secretly stopped providing its fastest delivery service to residents of two predominantly Black neighborhoods while still charging millions of dollars for a membership that promises the benefit.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump's FBI pick Kash Patel targeted in Iranian cyberattack
item.description: Kash Patel, President-elect Trump’s nominee to lead the FBI, was among those targeted by an Iranian cyber attack. It’s unclear whether any information was gained through the attack, but it makes Patel the latest figure in the Trump campaign to be pursued by the nation.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman 'not that worried' about rival Elon Musk's influence in the Trump administration
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, who is in a legal dispute with rival Elon Musk, said he is “not that worried” about Musk's influence in the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump nominates Paul Atkins as SEC chair
item.description: President-elect Trump has nominated former Securities and Exchange Commissioner (SEC) Paul Atkins to replace outgoing chair Gary Gensler when he departs on Inauguration Day. “Paul is a proven leader for common sense regulations.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Lawsuit alleges Amazon deliveries are slower in 2 majority-Black DC zip codes
item.description: A lawsuit from the Washington, D.C., attorney general is alleging that Amazon deliveries are slower in two majority-Black zip codes in the nation’s capital. The lawsuit, from D.C.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: The first ‘AI elections’ weren’t as disastrous as predicted. Here’s why
item.description: It’s been the biggest year for elections in human history: 2024 is a “super-cycle” year in which 3.7 billion eligible voters in 72 countries had the chance to go to the polls.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft hit with more litigation accusing it of predatory pricing
item.description: A UK law firm on Tuesday filed what amounts to a class-action lawsuit against Microsoft, accusing it of using its market share to overcharge clients running Windows Server on AWS, Google, or Alibaba cloud offerings instead of on Microsoft Azure.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AWS PartyRock updated with free daily usage for developers
item.description: Amazon Web Services has updated AWS PartyRock, its mostly free low code tool for building generative AI applications with a new app search function and the ability to build document processing into applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger retires
item.description: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger retired Sunday, after more than 40 years in the industry. The company quickly named two interim co-CEOs to hold the fort while it searches for a long-term replacement.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AWS brings RAG evaluation and LLM-as-a-judge feature to Amazon Bedrock
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has updated Amazon Bedrock with features designed to help enterprises streamline the testing of applications before deployment.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Artificial intelligence changes across the US
item.description: As more companies implement artificial intelligence for daily tasks, some industry leaders are working to push back against certain aspects of the technology.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two years of ChatGPT: the conversation that never ends
item.description: Two years can be an eternity for the technology industry: plenty of time for enterprises to innovate, launch a new product, peak on the stock market… and then plummet again. Think about the bubbles that briefly surrounded 3D printing, smart glasses, or the metaverse.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Siberia's 'Gateway to Hell' crater fuels new fears
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says the Batagaika crater in Siberia expands 35 million cubic feet yearly due to thawing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada sues Google over alleged anticompetitive practices in online ads
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada's antitrust watchdog said Thursday it is suing Google over alleged anticompetitive conduct in the tech giant’s online advertising business and wants the company to sell off two of its ad tech services and pay a penalty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
